untranslatable_reason: This signal requires a Kalman filter implementation to dynamically estimate the slope (β) of a linear regression between two cointegrated ETFs (EWC vs EWA), and to track deviations of the estimated slope from equilibrium (β=1). The allowed indicator set does not include: (1) Kalman filter state estimation, (2) dynamic linear regression slope tracking, (3) multi-asset cointegration analysis, (4) measurement prediction errors from state-space models. The signal also references parameters (lookback_period, threshold, half_life, kalman_process_noise) that are marked AUTHOR_SILENT, making entry/exit conditions incompletely specified. This requires custom algorithmic implementation beyond the scope of the allowed indicators.
translation_notes: Signal CHAN_AT_DRY_4 is a sophisticated pairs trading strategy from mean reversion literature that cannot be reduced to simple indicator thresholds. It requires: (a) real-time Kalman filtering on a state-space model, (b) cointegration testing and slope estimation between two separate ETF price streams, (c) threshold determination for 'significant deviation' from β=1. The source code snippet references Equation 3.7 and state covariance prediction (R=P+Vw), confirming the state-space filtering requirement. Recommend implementing as custom strategy module rather than rule-based backtest engine condition.
